message:
sync: Close bd-1jt.2 (Local Build Infrastructure) and bd-sfn (E2E health)

bd-1jt.2 criteria verified:
- hosts.yaml template has trj/mmini/wlap
- build_state.sh handles artifacts + manifests
- ACT_COMPATIBILITY.md documents cross-compilation
- --non-interactive flag implemented

bd-sfn criteria verified:
- E2E test e2e_health.sh validates all acceptance criteria

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -12,7 +12,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1jt.1.7","title":"Implement auto-tag from version files","description":"# Auto-Tag from Version Files

## Purpose
Automatically create git tags when version files (Cargo.toml, package.json, VERSION, etc.) are updated. This keeps releases consistent across tools and avoids manual tagging errors.

## Version Sources (configurable)
| Language | File | Pattern |
|----------|------|---------|
| Rust | Cargo.toml | version = "X.Y.Z" |
| Go | VERSION or version.go | Version = "X.Y.Z" |
| Bun/Node | package.json | "version": "X.Y.Z" |
| Python | pyproject.toml | version = "X.Y.Z" |

## Implementation Notes
- Use repo registry (`repos.d/<tool>.yaml`) to resolve `local_path`
- **No global cd** — use `git -C <repo>`
- Dirty tree detection via exit codes (`git diff-index --quiet HEAD --`)
- Tag existence via `git show-ref --tags -d "refs/tags/vX.Y.Z"`
- Support `--dry-run` and `--non-interactive`

## dsr Command
```bash
dsr version detect <tool>   # Show current version
dsr version tag <tool>      # Create tag if needed
dsr version tag --all       # Tag all repos with new versions
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detect version from all supported language files
- [ ] Skip if tag already exists
- [ ] Create annotated tags
- [ ] Push tags to remote
- [ ] All git operations use `git -C` (no global cd)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:28:57.429934130Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:03:38.381821252Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T19:03:38.381624401Z","close_reason":"Implemented auto-tag from version files: version.sh module + dsr version detect/tag commands","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:48.309988979Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:34:54.300293741Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.1.8","title":"Implement dsr config command and configuration management","description":"# dsr Configuration Management (XDG + Schema Validation)

## Purpose
Centralize configuration so every command uses the same sources of truth for repos, hosts, secrets, and defaults. Consistent config prevents drift and makes automation reproducible.

## XDG Layout
- `~/.config/dsr/`
  - `config.yaml` (global defaults + schema_version)
  - `repos.yaml` (tool registry + build matrix)
  - `hosts.yaml` (build hosts + capabilities)
  - `notifications.yaml`
- `~/.cache/dsr/` (API cache, downloads)
- `~/.local/state/dsr/` (logs, state, run history)

## Precedence Rules
1. CLI flags
2. Environment variables (`DSR_*`)
3. Config files
4. Built-in defaults

## repos.yaml Schema (minimum fields)
- `tool`, `repo`, `local_path`
- `language`, `build_cmd`, `binary_name`
- `targets` (os/arch list)
- `checks` (pre-release gates)
- `workflow` + `act_job_map`
- `artifact_naming` + `archive_format`

## hosts.yaml Schema
- host aliases, platform, capabilities, concurrency limits
- ssh options (connect timeout, identity)

## Migration & Versioning
- `schema_version` required
- `dsr config migrate` upgrades older schemas
- Unknown keys rejected unless `--allow-unknown`

## Commands
```bash
dsr config init
 dsr config show [key]
 dsr config validate
 dsr config set KEY=VALUE
 dsr config edit
 dsr config migrate
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Config loads at startup with deterministic precedence
- [ ] Validation catches missing/invalid keys
- [ ] Defaults are sane and documented
- [ ] `--json` outputs resolved config for automation
- [ ] yq-based edits are safe; fallback mode is read-only","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:23:27.935807062Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:57:12.840449840Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T15:47:07.588658283Z","close_reason":"Implemented dsr config command with init/show/get/set/validate/edit subcommands. Created src/config.sh module and main dsr CLI. XDG-compliant paths, JSON output support, precedence rules (CLI > env > config > defaults). Also implemented dsr doctor for diagnostics.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:33:56.274007404Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:38:53.251178068Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","depends_on_id":"bd-8cg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:50:48.532928914Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.1.9","title":"Implement GitHub API adapter with caching + rate-limit handling","description":"# Implement GitHub API Adapter with Caching + Rate-Limit Handling

## Purpose
Centralize all GitHub API access so commands are consistent, cached, and resilient to rate limits.

## Responsibilities
- Prefer gh api when available (auth + pagination)
- Fallback to curl + GITHUB_TOKEN
- Support ETag / If-None-Match caching in ~/.cache/dsr/github/
- Normalize errors into a common JSON shape
- Expose helpers for workflow runs, releases, tags, and compare endpoints

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unified helper used by dsr check/watch/release
- [ ] API responses cached with TTL + ETag
- [ ] Rate-limit errors trigger backoff + clear messages
- [ ] All errors map to global exit codes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:31.418006660Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T16:13:44.413349290Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T16:13:44.413268548Z","close_reason":"Implemented GitHub API adapter in src/github.sh with: gh_api() for unified API access (gh CLI or curl fallback), ETag caching in ~/.cache/dsr/github/, rate-limit handling with backoff, and helpers for workflow_runs, releases, tags, compare, and asset upload.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:31.418006660Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1jt.2","title":"Theme 2: Local Build Infrastructure","description":"# Local Build Infrastructure

## Purpose
Build binaries locally when GH Actions is throttled, using available hardware:
- Linux builds on Threadripper workstation (trj, local)
- macOS builds on Mac mini (mmini)
- Windows builds on Windows laptop (wlap) or cross-compilation fallback

## Build Hosts

### Threadripper Workstation (trj)
- Connection: local
- OS: Ubuntu Linux
- Arch: x86_64
- Can build: Linux x86_64, Linux ARM64 (via cross), Windows (via cross)
- Toolchains needed: Rust, Go, Bun, Docker/act

### Mac mini (mmini)
- Connection: ssh mmini (via Tailscale)
- OS: macOS
- Arch: ARM64 (Apple Silicon)
- Can build: macOS ARM64 (native), macOS x86_64 (via arch -x86_64)
- Toolchains needed: Rust, Go, Bun

### Windows laptop (wlap)
- Connection: ssh wlap (via Tailscale)
- OS: Windows 10/11
- Arch: x86_64
- Can build: Windows x86_64 (native)
- Toolchains needed: Rust, Go, Bun

## Cross-Compilation Strategy
For targets we can't build natively:
- Rust: cargo-zigbuild or cross for Windows/ARM
- Go: GOOS/GOARCH environment variables
- Bun: bun compile with target flag

## Key Commands
```bash
dsr build ntm                       # Build for all targets
dsr build ntm --target linux-amd64  # Specific target
dsr build ntm --host trj            # Force specific build host
```

## Target Matrix (example)

| Tool | Language | Linux x64 | Linux ARM | macOS ARM | macOS x64 | Windows |
|------|----------|-----------|-----------|-----------|-----------|---------|
| ntm  | Go       | trj       | trj/cross | mmini     | mmini     | wlap    |
| cass | Rust     | trj       | trj/cross | mmini     | mmini     | wlap    |
| bv   | Go       | trj       | trj/cross | mmini     | mmini     | wlap    |
| ...  | ...      | ...       | ...       | ...       | ...       | ...     |

## Build Output
Artifacts + logs live under the state directory:
- `~/.local/state/dsr/artifacts/<tool>/<version>/<target>/`
- `~/.local/state/dsr/builds/<tool>/<version>/<run_id>/` (per-host logs + metadata)

## Dependencies
- SSH access to build hosts (mmini, wlap)
- Toolchains installed on each host
- Docker + act available on trj for Linux workflows
- Sufficient disk space (~5GB per full build)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Host names and mappings align with `hosts.yaml` (trj/mmini/wlap)
- [ ] Artifacts and logs land in state directory with a manifest
- [ ] Cross-compilation paths documented for Rust/Go/Bun
- [ ] Non-interactive builds run without prompts","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:08:17.777193563Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:49:46.916624939Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:08:17.777193563Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.1","title":"Implement dsr build command - local compilation","description":"# Implement dsr build command — local + remote compilation

## Purpose
Compile a tool locally or on remote build hosts, producing release-ready binaries with a manifest and logs.

## Command Interface
```bash
dsr build <tool>
 dsr build <tool> --target <target>
 dsr build <tool> --host <host>
 dsr build <tool> --version v1.2.3
 dsr build <tool> --output-dir <dir>
 dsr build <tool> --parallel
 dsr build <tool> --resume
```

## Build Flow (deterministic)
1. Resolve repo config + tag/commit (no dirty tree unless --allow-dirty)
2. Acquire lock + create run_id
3. Select host per target (health + capability matrix)
4. Build via act for Linux jobs; native builds for macOS/Windows
5. Collect artifacts to state dir + manifest
6. Emit JSON summary

## Implementation Notes
- Use `git -C <repo>` for all git operations (no global cd)
- Use `ssh <host> "<cmd>"` with explicit error capture
- Use rsync/scp with retries for artifact collection
- Use manifest schema as source of truth for release

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Builds all targets for Rust/Go/Bun tools
- [ ] Artifacts named per manifest contract
- [ ] Logs captured per host
- [ ] Parallel builds do not collide
- [ ] Resume skips completed targets","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:08:35.463350565Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:11:50.526013804Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T19:11:50.525659928Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete in dsr cmd_build() (lines 1360-1638). Uses act_orchestrate_build for Linux, SSH for macOS/Windows. Supports --target, --version, --parallel, --resume, --allow-dirty. Outputs JSON manifest. Requires repos.yaml tool configs for full  {"id":"bd-1jt.2.10","title":"Validate GoReleaser config compatibility for each tool","description":"# GoReleaser Config Validation

## Purpose
Ensure dsr can replicate GoReleaser outputs (targets, archives, naming) so fallback builds match official releases.

## Approach
- Prefer `goreleaser release --snapshot --skip-publish` when available
- Fallback: parse .goreleaser.yaml via yq
- Compare produced artifacts against expected naming template

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Targets and archive formats match GoReleaser config
- [ ] Naming templates produce identical asset names
- [ ] Fallback parsing works when goreleaser not installed","notes":"Implemented goreleaser compatibility checks in dsr repos validate (targets, archive formats, name template). Shellcheck on dsr hung; killed process.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:21.603418246Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T20:01:57.246493773Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:21.603418246Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:49.305063723Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.11","title":"Implement build workspace isolation + lock/state machine","description":"# Implement Build Workspace Isolation + Lock/State Machine

## Purpose
Prevent overlapping builds and enable reliable resume/retry by persisting build state per tool/version.

## Key Behaviors
- Acquire lock per tool+version (state dir)
- Create unique run_id + workspace (no overwrites)
- Persist state JSON with per-host status + artifacts
- Resume uses state to skip completed hosts

## Requirements
- Explicit error handling (no `set -e`)
- State updated after every host step
- Locks released on clean exit or timeout
- Stale lock detection (PID + timestamp + TTL)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Concurrent runs are blocked or queued deterministically
- [ ] State file created and updated per host step
- [ ] --resume continues from last known state
- [ ] Logs reference run_id + state path
- [ ] Lock files are atomic and auto-cleaned when stale","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:39.000455353Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:40:21.024490854Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T17:40:21.024473411Z","close_reason":"Implemented build workspace isolation with lock/state machine in src/build_state.sh. 16 tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.11","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:39.000455353Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1jt.5.16","title":"Write tests for signing module (key mgmt + batch signing)","description":"## Purpose
Test the `src/signing.sh` module which provides minisign key management and artifact signing.

## Unit Tests (tests/unit/test_signing.bats)

### Key Management
- signing_init creates keypair with password protection
- signing_check fails when private/public key missing
- signing_fix_permissions sets correct mode (chmod 600)

### Signing Operations
- signing_sign creates .minisig file
- signing_verify validates signatures
- signing_sign_batch signs multiple files

### Error Handling
- signing_sign fails gracefully when minisign missing
- signing_verify fails on tampered file

## Logging Requirements
- All test failures must output full signing command and error
- Key paths logged for debugging permission issues
- Timing logged for batch operations

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unit tests for all signing_* functions
- [ ] Permission validation tests
- [ ] Batch signing tests with multiple files
- [ ] Error handling tests (missing minisign, bad perms, verification failures)
- [ ] Tests run in < 10 seconds
- [ ] Logs captured on failure","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:51:42.972843202Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:35:15.783971352Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:35:15.783461381Z","close_reason":"Added signing module tests (minisign stub)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.16","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:52:06.952317915Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.16","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:51:42.972843202Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.16","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.5.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:52:10.290497048Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.5.17","title":"Write tests for supply chain security (SLSA, SBOM, quality gates)","description":"# Tests for Supply Chain Security Features

## Purpose
Test SLSA provenance attestation, SBOM generation, and pre-release quality gates.

## Test Coverage

### SLSA Provenance Tests (tests/unit/test_slsa.bats)
```bash
@test "slsa_generate creates valid in-toto statement" {
  local artifact="$BATS_TEST_TMPDIR/test-binary"
  echo "binary" > "$artifact"
  
  run slsa_generate "$artifact" --builder "dsr/v1"
  assert_success
  
  # Verify JSON structure
  local stmt
  stmt="$(cat "${artifact}.intoto.jsonl")"
  assert_equal "$(echo "$stmt" | jq -r '._type')" "https://in-toto.io/Statement/v1"
  assert_equal "$(echo "$stmt" | jq -r '.predicateType')" "https://slsa.dev/provenance/v1"
}

@test "slsa_generate includes correct subject digest" {
  local artifact="$BATS_TEST_TMPDIR/test-binary"
  echo "binary content" > "$artifact"
  local expected_sha256
  expected_sha256="$(sha256sum "$artifact" | cut -d' ' -f1)"
  
  run slsa_generate "$artifact"
  assert_success
  
  local actual_sha256
  actual_sha256="$(cat "${artifact}.intoto.jsonl" | jq -r '.subject[0].digest.sha256')"
  assert_equal "$actual_sha256" "$expected_sha256"
}

@test "slsa_verify validates attestation" {
  # Create artifact + valid attestation
  local artifact="$BATS_TEST_TMPDIR/test-binary"
  echo "binary" > "$artifact"
  slsa_generate "$artifact"
  
  run slsa_verify "$artifact"
  assert_success
}

@test "slsa_verify fails on tampered artifact" {
  local artifact="$BATS_TEST_TMPDIR/test-binary"
  echo "binary" > "$artifact"
  slsa_generate "$artifact"
  
  # Tamper with artifact
  echo "modified" > "$artifact"
  
  run slsa_verify "$artifact"
  assert_failure
  assert_output --partial "digest mismatch"
}
```

### SBOM Tests (tests/unit/test_sbom.bats)
```bash
@test "sbom_generate creates SPDX JSON" {
  local project="$BATS_TEST_TMPDIR/project"
  mkdir -p "$project"
  echo '[package]
name = "test-crate"
version = "1.0.0"' > "$project/Cargo.toml"
  
  run sbom_generate "$project" --format spdx
  assert_success
  assert [ -f "$project/sbom.spdx.json" ]
  
  # Verify SPDX structure
  assert_equal "$(jq -r '.spdxVersion' "$project/sbom.spdx.json")" "SPDX-2.3"
}

@test "sbom_generate creates CycloneDX JSON" {
  local project="$BATS_TEST_TMPDIR/project"
  mkdir -p "$project"
  echo '{"name": "test-pkg", "version": "1.0.0"}' > "$project/package.json"
  
  run sbom_generate "$project" --format cyclonedx
  assert_success
  assert [ -f "$project/sbom.cdx.json" ]
}

@test "sbom_generate handles missing deps gracefully" {
  local project="$BATS_TEST_TMPDIR/empty"
  mkdir -p "$project"
  
  run sbom_generate "$project"
  assert_failure
  assert_output --partial "No package manifest found"
}
```

### Quality Gates Tests (tests/unit/test_quality_gates.bats)
```bash
@test "quality_gates runs configured checks" {
  # Mock check commands
  function cargo() { echo "Check passed"; return 0; }
  function go() { echo "Go vet passed"; return 0; }
  export -f cargo go
  
  run quality_gates "ntm" --config "$FIXTURES/repos-with-checks.yaml"
  assert_success
  assert_output --partial "All checks passed"
}

@test "quality_gates fails on blocking check failure" {
  function cargo() { echo "Error: unused import"; return 1; }
  export -f cargo
  
  run quality_gates "test-tool" --config "$FIXTURES/repos-with-checks.yaml"
  assert_failure
  assert_output --partial "Blocking check failed"
}

@test "quality_gates continues on allow_fail checks" {
  # lint fails but is in allow_fail
  function lint_tool() { return 1; }
  function test_tool() { return 0; }
  export -f lint_tool test_tool
  
  run quality_gates "test-tool" --config "$FIXTURES/repos-allow-fail.yaml"
  assert_success
  assert_output --partial "Non-blocking check failed: lint"
}

@test "quality_gates skipped with --skip-checks" {
  run quality_gates "ntm" --skip-checks
  assert_success
  assert_output --partial "Quality gates skipped"
}

@test "quality_gates produces JSON report" {
  run quality_gates "ntm" --json
  assert_success
  
  # Verify JSON structure
  echo "$output" | jq -e '.checks | length > 0'
  echo "$output" | jq -e '.passed == true'
}
```

### Integration Tests
```bash
@test "release includes SLSA + SBOM when enabled" {
  # Setup mock release
  run dsr release test-tool v1.0.0 --dry-run --with-slsa --with-sbom
  assert_success
  assert_output --partial "Would upload: test-tool.intoto.jsonl"
  assert_output --partial "Would upload: sbom.spdx.json"
}
```

## Fixtures
- tests/fixtures/repos-with-checks.yaml
- tests/fixtures/repos-allow-fail.yaml
- tests/fixtures/sample-cargo-project/
- tests/fixtures/sample-go-project/

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SLSA generation/verification tested
- [ ] SBOM generation for Rust/Go/Node tested
- [ ] Quality gates pass/fail/skip tested
- [ ] JSON reports validated

 {"id":"bd-1jt.5.18","title":"Schema + stream separation tests (JSON purity)","description":"# Schema + Stream Separation Tests (JSON Purity)

## Purpose
Guarantee that every `dsr --json` output is schema-valid and that stderr contains no human noise unless an error occurs.

## Test Files
- `tests/integration/test_json_envelope.bats`
- `tests/fixtures/` (command outputs + schema fixtures)

## Coverage
- `dsr check/watch/build/release/fallback/repos/status/report/prune/config/doctor --json`
- Validate against `schemas/envelope.json` + command-specific schemas
- Assert **stderr empty** in success mode

## Tooling
- Prefer `ajv` for full schema validation
- Fallback to `jq` structural checks when ajv missing

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each command’s JSON conforms to schema
- [ ] No stderr output in successful `--json` runs
- [ ] Failing commands include error envelope + non-zero exit

 {"id":"bd-1jt.5.2","title":"Write E2E tests for installer scripts using Docker","description":"# E2E Tests for Installer Scripts

## Purpose
Validate that generated install.sh scripts work correctly in real environments using Docker containers.

## Test File
`tests/e2e/test_installers.bats`

## Test Environments
- ubuntu:22.04 (Linux x64)
- debian:bookworm (alt Linux)
- arm64v8/ubuntu:22.04 (Linux ARM64, QEMU)

## Test Cases
- Basic install (binary download)
- Source build path (`--source`)
- Stale release detection
- Checksum verification
- Minisign verification (when available)
- Cache/offline mode (if enabled)
- Custom install prefix

## Logging
Each E2E test captures:
- Full Docker output
- Installer verbose logs
- Timing info

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Source build tests work
- [ ] Signature + checksum verification tested
- [ ] Cache/offline mode tested

Provide a clear, single entry-point doc for users and operators so dsr can be installed, configured, and used without reading internal plans.

- What dsr is + when to use it (GH Actions throttling)
- Quickstart: `dsr config init`, `dsr repos list`, `dsr check`, `dsr build`, `dsr release`, `dsr fallback`, `dsr doctor`, `dsr status`
- Config + registry layout (XDG + `repos.d/`)
- Build host expectations (trj/mmini/wlap) + toolchains
- Installer strategy + security notes (checksums/minisign)
- Exit codes table
- Logging/state locations and how to inspect failures
- Non-interactive + JSON modes (stream separation)
- Links to `docs/CLI_CONTRACT.md` and `docs/ACT_SETUP.md`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] README exists and is consistent with CLI contract
- [ ] Examples are copy-pasteable and current
- [ ] No contradictions with docs/CLI_CONTRACT.md
- [ ] Links to docs and config locations are  {"id":"bd-ov2","title":"Test infrastructure: coverage reporting and metrics","description":"# Test Infrastructure: Coverage Reporting and Metrics

## Purpose
Provide function-level and line-level coverage estimation for Bash scripts without external tools.

## Approach
Since Bash doesn't have built-in coverage tools like gcov, we use:
1. **Function enumeration**: Parse exported functions from each module
2. **Test mapping**: Track which tests call which functions
3. **Execution tracing**: Optional PS4-based tracing for line coverage

## Coverage Metrics

### 1. Function Coverage
- List all exported functions per module
- Track function calls during test execution
- Report: `functions_tested / functions_total`

### 2. Test-to-Function Mapping
```json
{
  "config.sh": {
    "config_init": ["test_config.sh::test_init"],
    "config_load": ["test_config.sh::test_load", "test_e2e.sh::test_full_flow"],
    "config_validate": []  // Not tested\!
  }
}
```

### 3. Gap Analysis
- Functions with zero test coverage
- Functions with only happy-path coverage
- Modules with low overall coverage

## Implementation

### Coverage Script
```bash
./scripts/coverage.sh [--module config.sh] [--format json|text]
```

### Output
```
=== Coverage Report ===
Module            Functions  Tested  Coverage
config.sh              8       6     75%
github.sh             10       7     70%
build_state.sh        15      15    100%
...
TOTAL                 50      42     84%

Untested functions:
- config.sh: config_validate
- github.sh: gh_create_release, gh_upload_asset, gh_delete_release
```

## CI Integration
- Run after test suite
- Fail if coverage drops below threshold
- Generate JSON for trending

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Parse exported functions from all src/*.sh
- [ ] Track function calls during test runs
- [ ] Generate human-readable report
- [ ] Generate JSON for CI
- [ ] Flag untested functions
- [ ] Optional threshold enforcement","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:35:27.277939425Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:49:24.894461697Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ov2","depends_on_id":"bd-130","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:35:27.277939425Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-sfn","title":"E2E: dsr health (real behavior)","description":"# E2E: dsr health (real behavior)

## Purpose
Verify host health checks run against real local host data and return schema-valid output.

## Approach
- Use temp hosts.yaml with one local host definition.
- Run `dsr health` and `dsr health check <host>`.

## Logging
- Record hosts config used.
- On failure, dump stdout/stderr and cache files.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Local host reports healthy (or actionable warnings).

- Run `dsr health` and `dsr health check <host>`.

## Logging
- Record hosts config used.
- On failure, dump stdout/stderr and cache files.

 {"id":"bd-v78","title":"Unit tests for toolchain_detect.sh module","description":"# Unit Tests for toolchain_detect.sh Module

## Module Overview
toolchain_detect.sh provides safe toolchain detection for installers:
- `toolchain_detect` - Detect specific toolchain (rust/go/bun/node)
- `toolchain_detect_all` - Detect all supported toolchains
- `toolchain_ensure` - Verify toolchain available
- `toolchain_install` - Install if missing (with consent)
- `_tc_version_ge` - Compare semver versions

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Detection Tests
- Detect Rust via `rustc --version`
- Detect Go via `go version`
- Detect Bun via `bun --version`
- Detect Node via `node --version`
- Handle missing toolchain gracefully

### 2. Version Comparison Tests
- 1.70.0 >= 1.70.0 → true
- 1.71.0 >= 1.70.0 → true
- 1.69.0 >= 1.70.0 → false
- Handle pre-release versions (1.70.0-beta)
- Handle missing version string

### 3. Platform Detection Tests
- Detect linux/amd64 correctly
- Detect darwin/arm64 correctly
- Detect windows/amd64 correctly
- Handle unknown platform

### 4. Installation Tests (with mocks)
- Prompt for consent in interactive mode
- Skip prompt in non-interactive mode
- Never overwrite existing installation
- Log installation commands (dry-run)

### 5. Ensure Tests
- Pass when toolchain present and version OK
- Fail when toolchain missing
- Fail when version too old
- Suggest installation command

## Test Approach
- Mock toolchain commands where needed
- Test version comparison logic directly
- Use NON_INTERACTIVE=true for consent bypass
- Verify no actual installations occur","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:34:04.144853096Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:00:05.378100130Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T19:00:05.377592634Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-v78","depends_on_id":"bd-130","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:34:04.144853096Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-z2t","title":"E2E: dsr release (real behavior)","description":"# E2E: dsr release (real behavior)

## Purpose
Validate release upload using real gh auth when available; otherwise skip with guidance.

## Approach
- Use a temp artifacts dir with small files and checksums.
- If gh auth present, target a test repo or draft release.
- If gh auth missing, skip with clear steps.

## Logging
- Log repo, tag, artifacts dir.
- On failure, dump gh output and stderr.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Draft release created and assets uploaded when auth present.
- [ ] Dry-run path produces deterministic plan output.
- [ ] --json output schema-valid.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:55:54.772796976Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T22:34:44.370102301Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T22:34:44.370085119Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-z2t","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:38.810809374Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-z2t","depends_on_id":"bd-f97","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:40.415849071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
